ATTICA. Athens. Ca. 440-404 BC. AR tetradrachm (25mm, 17.21 gm, 4h). NGC MS 5/5 - 5/5. Mid-mass coinage issue. Head of Athena right, wearing earring, necklace, and crested Attic helmet orname
sales.description:
Ancients
ATTICA. Athens. Ca. 440-404 BC. AR tetradrachm (25mm, 17.21 gm, 4h). NGC MS 5/5 - 5/5. Mid-mass coinage issue. Head of Athena right, wearing earring, necklace, and crested Attic helmet ornamented with three laurel leaves and vine scroll / AΘE, owl standing right, head facing; olive sprig with two leaves and one berry, and crescent moon behind, all within incuse square. Kroll 8. SNG Copenhagen 31-40. HGC 4, 1597. Glistening surfaces.  www.HA.com/TexasAuctioneerLicenseNotice

Ancients
TROAS. Ilium (Troy). Ca. 188-133 BC. AR tetradrachm (32mm, 16.86 gm, 11h). NGC AU 5/5 - 3/5. Head of Athena right, hair loose over shoulders, wearing crested and laureate Attic helmet / AΘHNAΣ / IΛIAΔOΣ / ANTIΦANOY / KΛE-ΩNOΣ, Athena Ilias advancing right on groundline, spear in right hand resting over right shoulder, distaff in left, wearing polos and chiton; owl standing right, head facing, to right. Bellinger in ANS MN 8, 18. Bellinger, Troy T 53.

sales.description:
Ancients
ATTICA. Athens. Ca. 510/500-480 BC. AR light-weight specimen tetradrachm (21mm, 16.38 gm, 11h). NGC VF 4/5 - 5/5, flan flaw. Head of Athena right, wearing crested Attic helmet, the crest box ornamented with chevron pattern / AΘΕ, owl standing right with closed wings, head facing; drooping olive sprig with two leaves and one berry behind, all within incuse square. Seltman Group C. HGC 4, 1590 (ca. 500/490-485/480 BC). A charming archaic rendition of the iconic owl.

Ex James E. Beach, ...

sales.title: Ira & Larry Goldberg Coins & Collectibles - Auction 150, Lot 475 — Attica, Athens. Ca. 440-404 BC. Silver Tetradrachm (17.18g), 23 mm. Hh. of Athena r. wearing crested helmet ornamented with three olive-leaves and floral scroll. Reverse: AOE, Owl stg. r. hd facing, i
sales.description:
Attica, Athens. Ca. 440-404 BC. Silver Tetradrachm (17.18g), 23 mm. Hh. of Athena r. wearing crested helmet ornamented with three olive-leaves and floral scroll. Reverse: AOE, Owl stg. r. hd facing, in erect posture; olive-twig and crescent; all within incuse square. S-2526. NGC graded Almost Unc; Strike: 4/5, Surface: 5/5. Athena exhibits an interesting tear drop below her eyelid! Struck in high relief and always in great demand. Lightly toned.  Estimated Value $1,250 - UP
Ex The Peter Preovo...
